missing_env_fallbacks.verdict: No env vars would crash the app if undefined. All 38 process.env accesses in packages/opencode/src have fallback patterns (if-guard, ||/??, === comparison, filter(Boolean)). The 4 without fallbacks (AICORE_DEPLOYMENT_ID, AICORE_RESOURCE_GROUP, 3×OTEL_ forwarding) are minor — they may pass undefined to APIs but won't crash.

items.reason: passed verbatim to child process env; undefined value forwarded
read without guard but only consumed conditionally

flag_import_time_risk.verdict: CRITICAL: 24 of 31 Flag entries are evaluated at module import time. env vars set after import are ignored.

config_loading_risks.verdict: HIGH RISK: Config file errors propagate as Effect defects (crash) not recoverable errors. Three distinct crash vectors.

items.issue: readConfigFile() pipes to Effect.orDie — any filesystem error (permissions, corrupt file, race condition) kills the fiber. If OPENCODE_CONFIG is set to a path that doesn't exist, the app crashes at startup.
Flag.OPENCODE_CONFIG loaded via loadFile() — if the path points to a non-existent or corrupt file, the error propagates through loadFile → readConfigFile → Effect.orDie → fiber crash

ConfigParse.jsonc() throws JsonError on parse failure (not returning an Effect). Called inside Effect.fnUntraced, so the throw becomes an unhandled defect. A config file with invalid JSONC kills the app.
ConfigParse.schema() throws InvalidError on schema validation failure. Same defect propagation issue. An unrecognized top-level key in config throws.

Global config file discovery reads opencode.jsonc, opencode.json, config.json in THAT order. But if none exist, it creates a default config.json with $schema. The first read failure (Effect.orDie) crashes before fallback to the next candidate.

path_resolution_issues.verdict: HIGH: 11 process.cwd() fallback locations cause silent wrong-directory behavior. 2 files with hardcoded '/' separators WILL fail on Windows.

windows_crashes.issue: Hardcoded forward slash concatenation produces mixed separators (e.g., 'C:\project/.rig/messages'). All downstream operations fail.
Same hardcoded forward slash via template literal. Infects 3 downstream file paths (lines 80-82).

cwd_fallbacks.usage: projectRoot used to normalize ALL file paths
Default layer uses cwd as project root
HTTP API fallback when no directory header

startup_init_order.verdict: MODERATE risk. Circular imports are avoided by Effect's lazy layer system. Main risks: (1) config.get() failure is uncaught in bootstrap, (2) service failures in forked fibers are silently swallowed, (3) module-level side effects execute at import time.

entry_points.description: Shell wrapper spawns platform binary with signal forwarding
Effect-based CLI handler builder with optional InstanceContext provisioning
Root runtime composing 50+ service layers via Layer.mergeAll

init_sequence: 1. Module loading: ESM import graph resolution with side effects
2. AppRuntime layer registration: 50+ default layers merged via Layer.mergeAll
3. Layer initialization: ManagedRuntime.make(AppLayer) triggers all layer constructors
4. InstanceLayer loads: dynamic import of bootstrap.ts (deferred)
5. InstanceStore.boot(): resolves project dir → runs InstanceBootstrap.run
6. InstanceBootstrap.run(): config.get() → plugin.init() → parallel init of 9 services
7. Service inits: each self-manages via Effect.forkScoped (failures = log warning only)

init_failure_issues.issue: config.get() has no error handling in bootstrap.run. If config loading fails (corrupt file, file not found), the entire instance boot fails.
plugin.init() has no error handling. If plugin initialization fails, the entire instance boot fails.
Service inits wrapped in Effect.catchCause(log warning) — failures are silently swallowed. snapshot.init(), lsp.init() etc can fail without the caller knowing.
readRuntimeFlags() runs Effect.runSync at function definition time. If RuntimeFlags layer fails, this throws at first caller.

module_side_effects.effect: Top-level await mkdir for 7 directories (data, config, state, tmp, log, bin, repos). Runs at import time, before any app initialization.
module_side_effects.risk: If ~/.opencode/ has permission issues, the module import itself fails

module_side_effects.effect: makeRuntime(Service, layer) creates ManagedRuntime at import time, triggering PubSub + Scope initialization eagerly.
module_side_effects.risk: Eager layer initialization before app is ready

module_side_effects.effect: makeRuntime(Service, defaultLayer) creates ManagedRuntime at import time for upgrade checks.
module_side_effects.risk: HTTP client initialized eagerly at import time

module_side_effects.effect: Bare import from @opencode-ai/core packages triggers their module initialization as side effect.
module_side_effects.risk: Side effects fire when the module graph resolves this file

platform_failures.verdict: CRITICAL gaps on ARM64 Linux and ARM64 Windows for binary downloads. macOS x64/ARM64 and Windows x64 are fully covered. Linux x64 is covered. Other platform handling is generally correct with minor gaps.

critical_items.issue: No 'linux-arm64' → 'arm64-linux' replacement in PLATFORM_KEY. 6 binaries (rg, fd, bat, delta, difft, eza) lack 'arm64-linux' platform entries. All binary downloads silently fail on ARM64 Linux (Raspberry Pi, Graviton).
PLATFORM_KEY replaces 'win32-arm64' → 'arm64-windows' but only rg has an arm64-windows entry. 5 of 6 binary downloads fail on Windows ARM64 (Snapdragon X, Surface Pro X).
getBackend() returns undefined for non-darwin/linux/win32 platforms (freebsd, aix, android). undefined passed to @parcel/watcher as backend argument.

minor_items.issue: Non-Windows hardcodes /bin/bash instead of /bin/sh or $SHELL. Fails on Alpine (ash), NixOS, etc.
Non-Windows extraction requires `unzip` in PATH. Fails on minimal Docker/Alpine images.
DesktopMenuPlatform type only includes 'macos' | 'windows'. Linux Electron builds get no custom menus.

database_drizzle_issues.verdict: MODERATE. Main concerns: PGlite WASM loading in bundled environments, data loss via :memory: default, and naming confusion (PGlite called 'SQLite' throughout).

items.issue: PGlite WASM binary must be loadable. In Electron ASAR or CSP environments, WASM fetch may fail with no fallback.
Migration path resolves via import.meta.url. In bundled environments (Electron ASAR, esbuild), this may resolve incorrectly.
Default connection string is ':memory:' — all data lost on restart unless OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL is explicitly set.

'SQLite' adapter wraps PGlite (PostgreSQL WASM), not SQLite. Transaction semantics differ (PGlite = serializable, not deferred/immediate/exclusive). Naming confusion could cause incorrect assumptions.
PGlite migration executes raw SQL per statement without Drizzle migration tracker. Partial migration leaves inconsistent state.

dependency_mismatches.verdict: All critical packages (effect, drizzle-orm, remeda) are unified via pnpm catalog references. No cross-package version mismatches found.

items.note: pre-release: breaking changes possible vs stable 1.0.0

critical_issues: CRITICAL: Binary downloads fail silently on ARM64 Linux and ARM64 Windows — 5 of 6 auxiliary binaries unsupported (manager.ts:23)
CRITICAL: readConfigFile() uses Effect.orDie — corrupt/missing config file kills the application (config.ts:391)
CRITICAL: ConfigParse.jsonc() throws on invalid JSONC — unhandled defect kills the application (parse.ts:27)
CRITICAL: send-message.ts:111 and read-messages.ts:79 use hardcoded '/' separators — crashes on Windows with mixed-separator paths
CRITICAL: 11 process.cwd() fallback locations produce silently wrong paths when not run from project root
HIGH: 24 of 31 Flag entries evaluated at module import time — env vars set after import are silently ignored
HIGH: config.get() and plugin.init() in bootstrap.ts have no error handling — any failure crashes the entire instance boot
HIGH: Module-level side effects (global.ts mkdir, bus/index.ts makeRuntime) run at import time before app is ready
MODERATE: Default DB connection is ':memory:' — all data lost on restart unless OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL is explicitly set
MODERATE: PGlite (called 'SQLite' in code) has fundamentally different transaction semantics from real SQLite
MODERATE: PGlite WASM may fail to load in bundled/Electron environments with no fallback
MINOR: Hardcoded /bin/bash path breaks on Alpine/NixOS Linux
MINOR: Non-Windows extraction requires `unzip` binary in PATH
MINOR: No Linux custom desktop menus in packages/app
out_of_scope_findings_recorded: send-message.ts:111 — hardcoded forward slash path separator on Windows
binary/manager.ts:23 — missing ARM64 platform entries for binary downloads
config/config.ts:391 — readConfigFile uses Effect.orDie kills fiber on read failure
flag/flag.ts:15 — 24 of 31 env flags evaluated at module import time
